project: Local Factory Queue and Worker Commands
description: Add a local-first, file-backed FIFO factory queue under the global Hal config directory, with commands to enqueue runs, list queue state as JSON, and process one queued run at a time through the existing factory executor.

userStories.title: Define Factory Queue JSON Contracts
userStories.description: As an integration author, I want documented queue JSON contracts so that automation can safely consume queue command output.
acceptanceCriteria: A queue entry contract documents stable fields for queue ID, run ID, executor mode, status, created timestamp, claimed timestamp, completed timestamp, claim metadata, attempt count, and last error.
`queue add`, `queue list --json`, and `queue work --json` response shapes are documented in `docs/contracts/`.
Example JSON payloads are added for add, list, work-claimed, and work-noop results using the repository's existing examples convention.
Contract tests assert the expected JSON key names for queue entry and queue command response structs.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Queue Storage Model
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want factory queue state stored under the global Hal config directory so that queued work survives CLI exits and restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: Factory queue storage behavior is implemented in `internal/factory/queue.go`.
Queue state is read from and written under the global Hal config directory, not the project `.hal/` directory.
Loading a missing queue file returns an empty queue without error.
Corrupt queue JSON returns a clear error and does not overwrite or delete the corrupt file.
Unit tests cover missing-file load behavior and corrupt-file handling.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist Queue Mutations Atomically
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want queue mutations to be atomic and locally guarded so that concurrent commands do not corrupt queue state.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue writes use a temp-file plus rename persistence pattern.
Queue mutation is protected against concurrent local process writes using a lock or equivalent deterministic guard.
A failed write does not leave a partially written queue file as the active queue state.
Unit tests cover atomic write behavior and concurrent mutation protection.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement FIFO Queue Operations
userStories.description: As a factory worker, I want queued entries returned and claimed in FIFO order so that local scheduling is deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: Enqueue creates exactly one queue entry containing queue ID, run ID, executor mode, status, created timestamp, and attempt count.
Queue entries are listed in FIFO order by creation time.
Claiming selects the oldest entry with `queued` status.
Unit tests use injected time and ID sources to cover enqueue behavior, FIFO listing, and FIFO claim selection.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Support Queue Entry Lifecycle State
userStories.description: As a worker process, I want queue entries to move through explicit lifecycle states so that interrupted and failed work can be inspected and retried later.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue entries support `queued`, `claimed`, `succeeded`, and `failed` states.
Claiming an entry records claim time and worker identity or process metadata.
Successful completion records completion time and either removes the entry from active queued work or marks it terminal according to the documented contract.
Failed completion records failure time, increments attempt count, stores the error message, and leaves the entry inspectable through JSON output.
Reloading queue state after a process restart preserves claimed and failed entries.
Unit tests cover successful claim, success completion, failed completion, retained failure state, and restart-safe reload behavior.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Register Factory Queue Command Group
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want queue commands grouped under `hal factory queue` so that factory scheduling commands are discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: `hal factory queue` is registered under the existing factory command group.
Queue command implementation lives in `cmd/factory_queue.go`.
The only edits to existing `cmd/factory.go` are adding the `queue` command registration and any required flag or help wiring for that registration.
Cobra help includes short descriptions and examples for `add`, `list`, and `work`.
Command metadata tests cover queue command help text and examples when existing metadata coverage requires it.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Queue Add Command
userStories.description: As a factory operator, I want to enqueue an existing factory run ID with an executor mode so that it can be processed later by a worker.
acceptanceCriteria: `hal factory queue add` accepts a run ID and executor mode.
The command creates one FIFO queue entry referencing the supplied run ID and executor mode.
The command rejects a missing run ID with a non-zero exit and actionable error.
The command rejects a missing executor mode with a non-zero exit and actionable error.
The command validates executor mode against modes supported by the current factory executor layer.
The command emits the documented machine-readable JSON shape when `--json` is provided.
The command updates run/event state to record that the run was queued.
Command tests cover valid add, missing run ID, missing executor mode, invalid executor mode, JSON output, and run/event state update.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Queue List JSON Command
userStories.description: As an automation consumer, I want to list queued and failed factory work as JSON so that local tooling can inspect pending work deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: `hal factory queue list --json` returns the documented JSON payload containing queue entries in FIFO order.
The JSON output includes queued, claimed, and failed entries by default.
Empty queue output is valid JSON with an empty entries array.
The command avoids human-only formatting when `--json` is set.
Command tests assert exact JSON keys, empty queue output, and deterministic ordering.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Queue Work Claiming
userStories.description: As a factory worker, I want `queue work` to claim one queued run at a time so that local processing is deterministic and bounded.
acceptanceCriteria: `hal factory queue work` claims at most one `queued` entry per invocation by default.
Claiming is atomic with respect to other concurrent workers.
If no queued entries exist, the command exits successfully.
When no queued entries exist and `--json` is provided, the command emits the documented no-work JSON response.
The command updates run/event state when a run is claimed.
Tests cover no-work behavior, single-entry claim, FIFO claim order, concurrent claim safety, and claimed run/event state update.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Execute Claimed Queue Entries
userStories.description: As a factory operator, I want queued work to run through the existing factory execution path so that queue processing preserves current run semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: `hal factory queue work` invokes the existing factory run executor path for the claimed run ID and executor mode.
The queue worker updates run/event state through the same internal execution path used by direct factory runs and does not introduce a separate run/event persistence path.
A successful executor result records success in both run/event state and queue state.
A failed executor result records failure in both run/event state and queue state.
Failed entries are not removed from inspectable queue state.
Worker tests inject executor dependencies and do not invoke real external CLIs.
Tests cover executor success, executor failure, queue success state, queue failure state, and run/event updates for both outcomes.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Deterministic Queue Test Coverage
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want deterministic queue test coverage so that scheduling semantics do not regress.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue storage tests use temporary directories and injected time or ID sources where needed.
Command tests capture stdout and stderr with buffers instead of depending on terminal state.
Worker tests inject executor behavior for success, failure, and no-op cases.
Queue tests do not require network access, GitHub access, Codex CLI execution, or sandbox providers.
`go test ./...` passes.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
